name: ASP_SERVER
author: tomous.wega@gmail.com

installation.script:
#!/bin/ash

# AdvancedSlimePaper Installation Script (Final Version)
# Server Files: /mnt/server

API_URL="https://api.infernalsuite.com/v1/projects/asp"
BRANCH_NAME="main"
TARGET_MC_VERSION="${MINECRAFT_VERSION}"
SERVER_JARFILE="${SERVER_JARFILE:-server.jar}"

echo "Fetching ASP builds from API..."

# Get all builds
BUILDS_JSON=$(curl -s "$API_URL")

if [ -z "$BUILDS_JSON" ]; then
    echo "Error: Could not fetch builds from ASP API."
    exit 1
fi

# Filter builds by branch (main) and optionally by MC version
if [ -n "$TARGET_MC_VERSION" ]; then
    echo "Looking for main branch builds with MC version: $TARGET_MC_VERSION"
    FILTERED_BUILDS=$(echo "$BUILDS_JSON" | jq --arg branch "$BRANCH_NAME" --arg mcver "$TARGET_MC_VERSION" '[.[] | select(.branch == $branch and (.mcVersion[]? == $mcver))]')
else
    echo "Looking for latest main branch build..."
    FILTERED_BUILDS=$(echo "$BUILDS_JSON" | jq --arg branch "$BRANCH_NAME" '[.[] | select(.branch == $branch)]')
fi

# Check if we found any builds
BUILD_COUNT=$(echo "$FILTERED_BUILDS" | jq 'length')
if [ "$BUILD_COUNT" -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "No builds found for branch '$BRANCH_NAME'"
    if [ -n "$TARGET_MC_VERSION" ]; then
        echo "with MC version '$TARGET_MC_VERSION'. Trying latest main branch build..."
        FILTERED_BUILDS=$(echo "$BUILDS_JSON" | jq --arg branch "$BRANCH_NAME" '[.[] | select(.branch == $branch)]')
        BUILD_COUNT=$(echo "$FILTERED_BUILDS" | jq 'length')
        if [ "$BUILD_COUNT" -eq 0 ]; then
            echo "No main branch builds found at all!"
            exit 1
        fi
    else
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Get the latest build
LATEST_BUILD=$(echo "$FILTERED_BUILDS" | jq '.[0]')
BUILD_ID=$(echo "$LATEST_BUILD" | jq -r '.id')
MC_VERSION=$(echo "$LATEST_BUILD" | jq -r '.mcVersion[0]')
BUILD_DATE=$(echo "$LATEST_BUILD" | jq -r '.date')

echo "Selected build:"
echo "  Build ID: $BUILD_ID"
echo "  MC Version: $MC_VERSION"
echo "  Date: $BUILD_DATE"

# Look specifically for asp-server.jar
SERVER_FILE=$(echo "$LATEST_BUILD" | jq -r '.files[] | select(.fileName == "asp-server.jar") | .id')

if [ -z "$SERVER_FILE" ]; then
    # Fallback: look for any file with "server" in the name
    SERVER_FILE=$(echo "$LATEST_BUILD" | jq -r '.files[] | select(.fileName | test("server"; "i")) | .id' | head -n1)
fi

if [ -z "$SERVER_FILE" ]; then
    echo "Error: Could not find asp-server.jar or any server file"
    echo "Available files:"
    echo "$LATEST_BUILD" | jq -r '.files[] | "  - $.fileName) (ID: $.id))"'
    exit 1
fi

FILE_ID="$SERVER_FILE"
echo "Selected server file ID: $FILE_ID"

# Show file details
SELECTED_FILE=$(echo "$LATEST_BUILD" | jq -r --arg fileid "$FILE_ID" '.files[] | select(.id == $fileid)')
FILE_NAME=$(echo "$SELECTED_FILE" | jq -r '.fileName')
echo "File details: $FILE_NAME"

# Construct download URL
DOWNLOAD_URL="https://api.infernalsuite.com/v1/projects/asp/$BUILD_ID/download/$FILE_ID"

cd /mnt/server

echo ""
echo "Downloading ASP server jar from: $DOWNLOAD_URL"

# Backup existing jar if it exists
if [ -f "$SERVER_JARFILE" ]; then
    mv "$SERVER_JARFILE" "${SERVER_JARFILE}.old"
fi

# Download the new jar
curl -o "$SERVER_JARFILE" "$DOWNLOAD_URL"

if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "Error: Failed to download ASP jar"
    exit 1
fi

# Check the downloaded file
echo ""
echo "Downloaded file info:"
ls -la "$SERVER_JARFILE"

# Download server.properties if it doesn't exist
if [ ! -f server.properties ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "Downloading MC server.properties"
    curl -o server.properties https://raw.githubusercontent.com/parkervcp/eggs/master/minecraft/java/server.properties
fi

# Pull start script from GitHub
echo ""
echo "Pulling start script '${START_SCRIPT}' from GitHub repo"

# Always start clean - remove any broken git setup
rm -rf .git 2>/dev/null || true

if [[ -n "${GIT_SCRIPT_USER}" && -n "${GIT_SCRIPT_ACCESS_TOKEN}" ]]; then
    AUTH_GIT_URL="https://${GIT_SCRIPT_USER}:${GIT_SCRIPT_ACCESS_TOKEN}@${GIT_SCRIPT_URL#https://}"
else
    AUTH_GIT_URL="${GIT_SCRIPT_URL}"
fi

if command -v git >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "Git available - cloning start script from repo"
    git -c init.defaultBranch="${GIT_SCRIPT_BRANCH}" init
    git config --global --add safe.directory "$PWD"
    git remote add origin "${AUTH_GIT_URL}"
    git fetch origin "${GIT_SCRIPT_BRANCH}"
    git checkout FETCH_HEAD -- "${START_SCRIPT}"
else
    echo "Git not available - using raw download"
    REPO_PATH=$(echo "${GIT_SCRIPT_URL}" | sed -e 's|https://github.com/||' -e 's|\.git$||')
    RAW_URL="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/${REPO_PATH}/${GIT_SCRIPT_BRANCH}/${START_SCRIPT}"
    if [[ -n "${GIT_SCRIPT_USER}" && -n "${GIT_SCRIPT_ACCESS_TOKEN}" ]]; then
        curl -u "${GIT_SCRIPT_USER}:${GIT_SCRIPT_ACCESS_TOKEN}" -L -o "${START_SCRIPT}" "$RAW_URL"
    else
        curl -L -o "${START_SCRIPT}" "$RAW_URL"
    fi
fi

# Clean up git after successful download
rm -rf .git 2>/dev/null || true

if [ -f "${START_SCRIPT}" ]; then
    echo "Start script '${START_SCRIPT}' downloaded successfully."
    chmod +x "${START_SCRIPT}"
else
    echo "Failed to download start script '${START_SCRIPT}'."
    exit 1
fi

echo ""
echo "Done. ASP server installed successfully!"
echo "MC Version: $MC_VERSION"
echo "Build ID: $BUILD_ID"
echo "Server JAR: $FILE_NAME"

variables.name: Minecraft Version
variables.description:
The version of minecraft to download. 

Leave at latest to always get the latest version. Invalid versions will default to latest.

variables.name: Server Jar File
variables.description: The name of the server jarfile to run the server with.

variables.name: Start Script
variables.description: Name of the start script to pull from your GitHub repository.

variables.name: Git Script URL
variables.description: HTTPS URL of the GitHub repository hosting the start script.

variables.name: Build Directory
variables.description: Directory path where the server files reside and where git operations will be performed.

variables.name: Git Pull URL
variables.description: HTTPS URL of the GitHub repo used to pull server files.

variables.name: Git Pull Branch
variables.description: Git branch to pull server files from

variables.name: Git Pull User
variables.description: GitHub username with access to the server files repo (for private repos). Optional.

variables.name: Git Pull Access Token
variables.description: GitHub Personal Access Token with read permission to the server files repo (for private repos). Optional.

variables.name: Git Script Branch
variables.description: Git branch containing the start script (default: main).

variables.name: Git Script User
variables.description: GitHub username with access to the start script repository (for private repos). Optional.

variables.name: Git Script Access Token
variables.description: GitHub Personal Access Token with read permission to the start script repository (for private repos). Optional.

variables.name: Download Path
variables.description: A URL to use to download a server.jar rather than the ones in the install script. This is not user viewable.

variables.name: Build Number
variables.description:
The build number for the paper release.

Leave at latest to always get the latest version. Invalid versions will default to latest.

variables.name: Server ID
variables.description: Unique ID for the server to use in config files

variables.name: Git Script Mode
variables.description:
How the git script should work. Options:
- disabled (nothing, just starts the server)
- pull (fetches the server files from github and deletes the files on shutdown)
- first-pull (fetches the server files from github only once and then disables and future pulling)
